steps.title: Identify the passage's direct statement of what Herodotus wrote
steps.content: The passage explicitly states what Herodotus included in his Histories: detailed descriptions of chambers, courts, and passages that he characterized as “an unending marvel”—an account of the complex's magnificence.

steps.title: Apply this description to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A matches exactly—it states Herodotus included “an account of the magnificence of the complex that once stood beneath the pyramid at Hawara.” The other choices reference information absent from the passage: construction time (B), how building caused prosperity (C), and how Herodotus arranged his visit (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
